2603.18641.title: A Comparative Empirical Study of Catastrophic Forgetting Mitigation in Sequential Task Adaptation for Continual Natural Language Processing Systems
2603.18641.paper_summary: Neural language models deployed in real-world applications must continually adapt to new tasks and domains without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. This work presents a comparative empirical study of catastrophic forgetting mitigation in continual intent classification. Using the CLINC150 dataset, we construct a 10-task label-disjoint scenario and evaluate three backbone architectures: a feed-forward Artificial Neural Network (ANN), a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and a Transformer encoder, under a range of continual learning (CL) strategies. We consider one representative method from each major CL family: replay-based Maximally Interfered Retrieval (MIR), regularization-based Learning without Forgetting (LwF), and parameter-isolation via Hard Attention to Task (HAT), both individually and in all pairwise and triple combinations. Performance is assessed with average accuracy, macro F1, and backward transfer, capturing the stability-plasticity trade-off across the task sequence. Our results show that naive sequential fine-tuning suffers from severe forgetting for all architectures and that no single CL method fully prevents it. Replay emerges as a key ingredient: MIR is the most reliable individual strategy, and combinations that include replay (MIR+HAT, MIR+LwF, MIR+LwF+HAT) consistently achieve high final performance with near-zero or mildly positive backward transfer. The optimal configuration is architecture-dependent. MIR+HAT yields the best result for ANN and Transformer, MIR+LwF+HAT, on the other hand, works the best for GRU, and in several cases CL methods even surpass joint training, indicating a regularization effect. These findings highlight the importance of jointly selecting backbone architecture and CL mechanism when designing continual intent-classification systems.
2603.18641.paper_summary_zh: 神經語言模型在實際應用中必須不斷適應新任務和領域，而不會忘記先前獲得的知識。這項工作呈現了一項有關持續意圖分類中災難性遺忘緩解的比較實證研究。使用CLINC150數據集，我們構建了一個10任務標籤不重疊的場景，並在一系列持續學習（CL）策略下評估三種主幹架構：前饋人工神經網絡（ANN）、門控循環單元（GRU）和Transformer編碼器。我們考慮了每個主要CL家族中的一種代表性方法：基於重播的最大干擾檢索（MIR）、基於正則化的無遺忘學習（LwF）以及通過硬注意力對任務進行參數隔離（HAT），這些方法分別以及所有成對和三重組合進行評估。性能評估使用平均準確率、宏F1和向後轉移，捕捉任務序列中的穩定性-可塑性權衡。我們的結果顯示，天真的序列微調對所有架構都會遭受嚴重的遺忘，且沒有單一的CL方法能完全防止這種情況。重播成為一個關鍵成分：MIR是最可靠的單一策略，而包含重播的組合（MIR+HAT、MIR+LwF、MIR+LwF+HAT）始終能以接近零或輕微正向的向後轉移達到高最終性能。最佳配置依賴於架構。對於ANN和Transformer，MIR+HAT產生了最佳結果；而MIR+LwF+HAT則在GRU上表現最佳，在幾個案例中，CL方法甚至超越了聯合訓練，顯示出正則化效應。這些發現強調了在設計持續意圖分類系統時，聯合選擇主幹架構和CL機制的重要性。
2603.18641.author: Aram Abrahamyan et.al.
